summary: Complete FR-1.4 cross-file OpenSCAD symbol completion by connecting the C6 in-memory project snapshot to the editor and structurally following project include/use references without granting filesystem access.
specSections: Section 4.1 C1 editor and language support
Section 4.6 C6 files, projects, import/export, sharing

decisions.decision: Supply CodeEditor with a read-only text map built from the C6 in-memory project snapshot and overlay every open text buffer before completion; exclude binary assets by type and retain the latest map behind a live completion-provider reference.
decisions.rationale: FR-1.4 must see saved and unsaved project source without reading the host filesystem, copying binary assets during UI rendering, or leaving CodeMirror with stale symbols after project state changes.

decisions.topic: Structural include/use traversal and symbol precedence
decisions.decision: Parse include/use statements with the fresh Lezer grammar, resolve only exact normalized project-relative paths from the injected map, traverse references recursively with cycle, file-count, and per-source bounds, expose variables and callables through include but only callables through use, and order declarations as current file over project over built-ins.
decisions.rationale: Structural traversal avoids comment and string false positives, OpenSCAD visibility rules prevent use from leaking project variables, bounded exact-map lookup contains hostile paths and cycles, and deterministic precedence keeps the closest user declaration authoritative.

testEvidence.test: Offer modules, functions, and variables from a structurally included project file while excluding an unrelated project file.

red.outcome: RED: 1 of 13 tests failed because the expected project module bracket(width = 10) was absent; 12 existing completion tests passed.

green.outcome: GREEN: all 13 completion tests passed after adding exact injected-map lookup and structural declaration extraction.

testEvidence.test: Follow transitive references without looping or accepting traversal paths, and expose project variables only through include rather than use.

red.outcome: RED: 2 of 15 tests failed because nested_part(size = 4) was absent from a transitive include and secret_value incorrectly leaked through use; 13 tests passed.

green.outcome: GREEN: all 15 completion tests passed with recursive visibility propagation, cycle detection, and unsafe-path rejection.

testEvidence.test: Install the injected project completion source in a mounted CodeMirror editor and read replacement project maps without reconstructing the editor.

red.outcome: RED: 1 of 8 tests failed because completion never became active for the referenced project module; 7 existing editor-language tests passed.

green.outcome: GREEN: all 8 editor-language tests passed, including a mounted completion result and a rerender that replaced bracket with brace without stale options.

testEvidence.test: Load a C6 project containing text and binary files through Workbench and complete a module from an included, unopened text file in the live editor.

red.command: node node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run tests/ui/Workbench.test.tsx --reporter=verbose -t "feeds the active C6 project snapshot into cross-file completion"
red.outcome: RED: the new Workbench integration test failed because CodeMirror completion status remained null; 18 unrelated Workbench tests were skipped by the filter.

green.outcome: GREEN: all 105 test files and 771 tests passed, including the focused completion, live-editor, Workbench, binary-exclusion, unsafe/cyclic-reference, include/use-visibility, and current-file-precedence cases.

testEvidence.test: Compose completion sources from saved project text plus authoritative open buffers without retaining binary assets or mutating the source snapshot.

red.outcome: RED: the new focused test failed because buildRuntimeTextFileMap did not yet exist.

green.outcome: GREEN: the focused runtime-map test passed, proving binary exclusion, open-buffer precedence, inclusion of an open text file, and snapshot immutability.

testEvidence.test: Keep cross-file completion composition outside the Workbench UI monolith and preserve the repository's 400-line UI ceiling.

red.command: node_modules/.bin/biome lint src tests scripts vite.config.ts playwright.config.ts && node scripts/check-source-policy.mjs
red.outcome: RED: Biome passed, but source policy rejected Workbench.tsx at 412 physical lines against the 400-line limit.

green.command: node_modules/.bin/biome.cmd lint src tests scripts vite.config.ts playwright.config.ts && node scripts/check-source-policy.mjs
green.outcome: GREEN: Biome checked 290 files with no fixes, and source policy passed after extracting text-map composition and its memoized editor context; Workbench.tsx is exactly 400 physical lines.

questions: Q-0014 remains open only for the historical M1 scheduling interpretation; M2 now supplies the conservative injected project-source map it proposed.
Q-0013 still parks only the final exhaustive built-in corpus and insertion text; this change does not alter that provisional corpus.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
